Class Event

java.lang.Object
dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.event.Event
Direct Known Subclasses:
Event_ContainerArrival, Event_ContainerValidation, Event_HashPowerChange, Event_NewTransactionArrival, Event_Report_BeliefReport, Event_Report_NodeStatusReport, Event_Report_StructureReport, Event_SeedUpdate, Event_TransactionPropagation
public class Event extends Object
Represents a single discrete occurrence within the event-driven simulation.

An Event encapsulates the notion of something happening at a particular simulation time. Subclasses specialize this class to define concrete simulation actions (for example, container arrivals, transaction propagation, etc.).

Each event maintains a unique identifier, a simulation time, and an optional flag indicating whether it should be ignored (useful for disregarding future events).

  • Field Details

    • currID

      public static long currID
      A global counter for assigning unique event IDs.

    • evtID

      public long evtID
      The ID of the current event object.

    • ignore

      protected boolean ignore
      If true, the event will be ignored when processed. Useful for disregarding future events.

  • Constructor Details

    • Event

      public Event()

  • Method Details

    • getNextEventID

      public static long getNextEventID()
      Retrieves the next unique event identifier.
      Returns:
      The next available event ID.

    • getEvtID

      public long getEvtID()
      Returns the unique identifier of this event instance. IDs are assigned at the time the event is processed.
      Returns:
      The event ID.

    • setTime

      public void setTime(long time)
      Sets the time of occurrence for this event.
      Parameters:
      time - The simulation time (must be non-negative).
      Throws:
      ArithmeticException - if time < 0.

    • getTime

      public long getTime()
      Returns the simulation time at which this event occurs.
      Returns:
      The event's simulation time.

    • ignoreEvt

      public boolean ignoreEvt()
      Indicates whether this event should be ignored by the simulator. Useful for canceling future events or suppressing redundant actions.
      Returns:
      true if the event should be ignored; false otherwise.

    • ignoreEvt

      public void ignoreEvt(boolean ignoreEvt)
      Sets whether this event should be ignored.
      Parameters:
      ignoreEvt - true to ignore the event, false otherwise.

    • happen

      public void happen(Simulation sim)
      Executes the event in the context of a simulation.

      This base implementation:

      • Assigns a unique event ID.
      • Triggers periodic reporting on all nodes based on the configured reporting window (see Config).
      • Triggers a time advancement report on each IMiner.
      Subclasses typically override this method to implement specific behaviors. Howver, Subclasses must call super.happen(sim) to allow the base functionality to execute. TODO: introduce template method pattern to enforce calling super.happen(sim)
      Parameters:
      sim - The active Simulation instance.

    • debugPrint

      public void debugPrint(String msg, Transaction tx, INode n, long tim, long delay)
      Prints a debug message describing the event and its associated transaction. This is intended for development and tracing only. TODO: remove the parameters and make it specific on the event data.
      Parameters:
      msg - A message prefix to display.
      tx - The relevant Transaction instance.
      n - The relevant IMiner instance.
      tim - The simulation time.
      delay - The delay (in milliseconds) before continuing execution.

    • debugPrint

      public void debugPrint(String msg, ITxContainer txc, INode n, long tim, long delay)
      Prints a debug message describing the event and its associated transaction container. This is intended for development and tracing only.
      Parameters:
      msg - A message prefix to display.
      txc - The relevant ITxContainer instance.
      n - The relevant IMiner instance.
      tim - The simulation time.
      delay - The delay (in milliseconds) before continuing execution.
